cards.back: A metric space is said to be locally arc-wise connected if for every x in the space and every epsilon > 0 there exists an open ball B(x, epsilon) such that the space is arc-wise connected in B(x, epsilon)
A metric space is said to be arc-wise connected if for every two points x and y in the space, there exists a continuous function f: [0,1] -> X such that f(0) = x and f(1) = y

A metric space is said to be locally connected if for every x in the space and every epsilon > 0, there exists a connected open set U containing x such that U is contained in the epsilon-ball B(x, epsilon)
The component of a point x in a metric space X is the largest connected open set containing x
A metric space is said to be path-connected if for every two points x and y in the space, there exists a continuous function f: [0,1] -> X such that f(0) = x and f(1) = y, and f is a path from x to y
